title: Delicate Comforting Turkish Daikon Kazandibi
description: A traditional Turkish dessert featuring daikon, kazandibi is a comforting and sweet treat, now adapted to be onion and garlic free. This recipe reduces the amount of sugar used in traditional kazandibi.
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine the grated daikon and milk, and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the daikon is tender.
In a small bowl, whisk together the sugar and cornstarch.
Add the sugar mixture to the saucepan with the daikon and milk, and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
Remove the saucepan from the heat, and stir in the melted butter, rose water, and lemon juice.
Pour the mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish, and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled.
Just before serving, use a fork to scrape the top of the kazandibi, creating a shaggy texture.
Serve the kazandibi chilled, garnished with a sprinkle of sugar if desired.
